sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Real Oviedo are struggling, winless in their last 6 matches, with only 2 draws and conceding heavily (20 goals this season, ranked 16th). Rayo Vallecano, on the other hand, have shown mixed form with 2 wins, 1 draw, and 2 losses in their last 5, scoring consistently (12 goals this season). Their attacking output gives them an edge going into this match.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last 5 head-to-head matchups, results are balanced with 1 win each and 3 draws, but Rayo Vallecano dominated the most recent encounter (4-1 in 2021). Historical trends show both teams often score, with 5 of 6 recent games having both teams on the scoresheet.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Real Oviedo, Salomon Rondon (2 goals) and Haissem Hassan (top dribbler, 2.5 per game) could be pivotal. Rayo Vallecano’s Jorge de Frutos (4 goals) and Alvaro Garcia (3 goals) are in fine form and likely to test Oviedo’s shaky defense.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Oviedo rank 20th in goals scored (7) and struggle with possession (17th, 43.6%) and big chance conversion (20th, 22.2%). Rayo Vallecano, at 14th, are more balanced, with better attacking stats and a knack for late goals (28.6% in 76-90 minutes), which could exploit Oviedo’s defensive lapses.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Oviedo may focus on defensive solidity (1st in clearances per game) but lack final third presence (20th in entries and passes). Rayo Vallecano’s ability to create big chances and maintain higher corner counts (often over 10.5 in recent games) suggests they could dominate set-pieces and transitions.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Rayo Vallecano’s superior form, attacking threat, and Oviedo’s defensive frailties, the Sofascore prediction (58% away win) seems plausible. Expect a competitive match, but Rayo’s momentum and historical edge should see them through, possibly with both teams scoring.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Overall half-time summary
halftimeSections.text: The first half between Real Oviedo and Rayo Vallecano at Carlos Tartiere ended in a goalless draw, with neither side able to break the deadlock. Real Oviedo showed more intent in attack, creating a few notable opportunities but failing to convert their chances. Rayo Vallecano, on the other hand, struggled to generate meaningful threats in the final third, often being pushed back by Oviedo's dominance in key areas. Possession was almost evenly split, but Oviedo's ability to win duels and maintain pressure in advanced positions gave them a slight edge.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Real Oviedo's performance
halftimeSections.text: Real Oviedo displayed a strong presence in the first half, particularly in their ability to enter the final third and create chances. They missed a significant opportunity to take the lead, with a big chance going begging. Their aerial dominance and success in duels allowed them to control much of the physical battle. Players like Santi Cazorla stood out with creative contributions, while Federico Viñas and Ilyas Chaira were active in testing the opposition defense. However, their finishing left much to be desired, as they hit the woodwork and squandered key moments.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Rayo Vallecano's performance
halftimeSections.text: Rayo Vallecano had a relatively quiet first half, failing to register any significant attempts on goal. Their entries into the final third were limited, and they struggled to win duels or assert control in the air. Defensively, they held firm under pressure but showed little ambition going forward. Players like Andrei Rațiu provided some stability at the back with important defensive contributions, but the attacking unit, including Sergio Camello and Álvaro García, remained largely ineffective. A yellow card also highlighted some disciplinary concerns for the away side.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The momentum in the first half swung predominantly in favor of Real Oviedo, especially during sustained spells in the middle and latter stages of the half, where they pushed forward with intent. Early on, Rayo Vallecano had brief moments of attacking pressure, but these quickly faded as Oviedo took control with consistent waves of attack. While Rayo managed a few surges late in the half, their efforts lacked the sharpness or frequency seen from the home side. Oviedo's persistent forward movement suggests they are more likely to dictate the tempo after the break.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key areas to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Real Oviedo, converting their chances will be crucial in the second half, as their wastefulness in front of goal could prove costly if Rayo finds a way back into the game. Their ability to maintain physical dominance and exploit the wings could open up spaces. Rayo Vallecano, meanwhile, needs to improve their transition play and find ways to involve their forwards more effectively. Defending set pieces will also be vital for Rayo, given Oviedo's strength in the air. Discipline could play a role, with Rayo already on a caution.
